summary: Cleaned up the Pyright baseline, restored reportArgumentType, reportOptionalSubscript, and reportAssignmentType to error severity, and wired Pyright into the normal automation validation chain by making make automation-check run make pyright before the Python tests. The remaining warning sites were fixed with honest optional parameter types, real argparse.Namespace fixtures in tests, and explicit narrowing for optional supervisor/simulator lookups. Pyright now reports 0 errors / 0 warnings without changing typeCheckingMode.

contract_status_changes.before: Pyright gate passed with 0 errors / 23 warnings and three diagnostics downgraded to warning.
contract_status_changes.after: Pyright gate passes with 0 errors / 0 warnings, the three diagnostics are restored to error, and make automation-check runs Pyright before Python automation tests.

residual_risks: make pyright remains available as a standalone target for narrow type-checking, but make automation-check now runs it by default.
recommended_next_reason: No next slice selected yet; rerun the supervisor after this commit lands.
